description: Sureños, also known as Southern United Raza, Sur 13 or Sureños X3, are loosely affiliated gangs that pay tribute to the Mexican Mafia in U.S. correctional facilities. Founded in 1967 in Southern California, they are primarily Mexican American and engage in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, and other crimes. They have a long-standing rivalry with Norteños and Nuestra Familia.

country_links.evidence_quote: They also can be found in some parts of Mexico. Sureños also maintain relationships with various drug trafficking organizations based in Mexico.
country_links.context: Sureños have been documented in some parts of Mexico and maintain relationships with Mexican drug cartels.

country_links.evidence_quote: Sureños have emerged as a national gang in the United States.
country_links.context: The Sureños operate as a national gang with presence in multiple U.S. states and in U.S. military bases.
